bead.title: docs: run Vale-backed prose pass across DDx docs
bead.description:
PROBLEM
After the Vale-backed checker, skill, and workflow hook work, DDx docs need one measured cleanup pass that applies high-signal prose findings and records the before/after result.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:361-369 says full-doc cleanup must wait until checker, skill, and workflow evidence paths are working. The cleanup needs to record finding count and word-count delta instead of silently rewriting docs.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Corpus-scale editorial cleanup is high-judgment. The agent must preserve DDx technical meaning, commands, paths, IDs, acceptance criteria, and rationale while reducing filler and AI-slop constructions where safe.

PROPOSED FIX
- Run the Vale-backed `ddx doc prose` pass across `docs/**`.
- Apply only high-signal findings.
- Preserve DDx terminology, commands, paths, headings, tables, frontmatter, IDs, AC, and useful technical density.
- Record before/after finding count and word-count delta in the bead evidence or close notes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not tune checker rules in this bead.
- Do not rewrite code comments outside docs.
- Do not pursue low-confidence findings.

DEPS
Depends on workflow-hook evidence so cleanup uses the final integrated prose workflow.
bead.acceptance:
1. `ddx doc prose docs` before cleanup records the baseline finding count.
2. High-signal findings are edited without changing DDx commands, paths, IDs, frontmatter, headings, tables, or acceptance criteria.
3. `ddx doc prose docs` after cleanup records the final finding count.
4. Word-count delta is recorded, and any increases are justified by added specificity.
5. `ddx doc audit` passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
